codeReview.summary: Review found one blocking correctness issue in the OpenAIRE connector. The implementation hard-codes the legacy /search/publications endpoint and unconditionally decodes JSON, but the real OpenAIRE API returns XML by default there, so live OpenAIRE searches fail instead of producing normalized records.

issues.description: The OpenAIRE connector points at https://api.openaire.eu/search/publications and then calls executeJSON on the response (lines 464-500). In practice that endpoint returns XML by default, even when sent Accept: application/json, so real searches fail with a JSON decode error and the feature does not make OpenAIRE 'reachable through the shared connector contract' as required. The added test only mocks a JSON payload for this endpoint (internal/connectors/second_wave_search_test.go:267-290), so it masks the production failure. The reference implementation handles OpenAIRE via an XML-first search flow and only uses the legacy publications endpoint as a fallback with explicit format=json.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state does not capture that OpenAIRE search responses are format-sensitive: the legacy /search/publications endpoint returns XML by default, and the reference implementation uses an XML-first flow before any JSON fallback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/connectors/second_wave.go:464-500 assumes JSON from /search/publications; a live GET to https://api.openaire.eu/search/publications?keywords=climate+science&size=1 returned content-type application/xml during review; openags/paper-search-mcp/paper_search_mcp/academic_platforms/openaire.py uses /search/researchProducts XML parsing first and only falls back to /search/publications with format=json.

summary: Failed review: the OpenAIRE connector is not production-correct because it expects JSON from an endpoint that serves XML by default, so OpenAIRE searches will fail outside the mocked test setup.
